reason: Modifying /etc/fstab to add a mount option changes the filesystem configuration. An incorrect edit can prevent /home from mounting, cause boot failures, or lock out users that rely on /home, which aligns with the policy that such partitioning/filesystem changes are considered too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential boot failure if fstab syntax is incorrect; Loss of access to /home causing user login issues; Impact on services that depend on /home directories; Possible system instability after remount
